Product update · UPD-001

Dual-Host Local-First MVP

The formal promise-to-evidence specification for a GitHub Pages web workspace and portable Electron local-file workspace over one shared product core.

TL;DR

4 promises, 14 scenarios, 31 user-can statements, 15 task flows, and 56 objective behaviors define this update. Status: implemented. The verified f29c9f9 MVP remains the immutable baseline.

Implemented · release verification pending

RUN-UPD001-001 passes local production web, packaged Electron, cross-host, migration, recovery, accessibility, regression, and data-custody checks. Actual GitHub Pages and clean-machine Windows evidence remain required.

Specification locked · LOCK-UPD-001-002

Scope, decisions, behavior, architecture, compatibility, quality scenarios, and release gates are stable. Later reviews are delta-only. Reopen only for evidence-backed or constraint-driven change whose product-aligned benefit exceeds full change and revalidation cost.

Customer promises

CP-WEB-LOCAL-WORKSPACE

A developer can open the GitHub Pages product without installation, manage ordinary projects locally, and continue without an application server.

web implemented

CP-DESKTOP-PORTABLE-WORKSPACE

A developer can run a portable Electron edition locally and use ordinary project folders and export folders without repetitive browser downloads.

desktop implemented

CP-CROSS-HOST-PORTABILITY

A developer can move the same project between web and desktop without changing its creative meaning, provenance, or generated output.

cross-host implemented

CP-LOCAL-DATA-CUSTODY

A developer retains custody of project and art data without an account or silent upload.

shared implemented

Host coverage

8

Web flows

4

Desktop flows

2

Cross-host flows

10

Deliberate exclusions

Task flows

IDHostUser completionStatus
TF-WEB-PROJECT-LIBRARYwebBrowser project library manages several projects without cross-project mutationimplemented
TF-WEB-AUTOSAVE-REOPENwebSemantic edit persists transactionally and restores exactlyimplemented
TF-WEB-LEGACY-MIGRATIONwebA valid version-1 project exists once as a parity-checked version-2 graph or the untouched legacy bytes remain recoverableimplemented
TF-WEB-SNAPSHOT-RESTOREwebThe selected snapshot becomes a new current revision while the pre-restore graph remains recoverableimplemented
TF-WEB-STORAGE-PRESSUREwebSafe cleanup or backup completes without silent loss of current, owned, referenced, migration-recovery, or last-known-good dataimplemented
TF-WEB-SAVE-CONFLICTwebA stale tab cannot overwrite the newer revision without one explicit recovery choiceimplemented
TF-WEB-BACKUP-RESTOREwebArchive restores the project after browser project storage is clearedimplemented
TF-WEB-OFFLINEwebInstalled project workflow and exports complete offlineimplemented
TF-DESKTOP-PORTABLE-LAUNCHdesktopDesktop host opens with correct capabilities and recent-folder stateimplemented
TF-DESKTOP-PROJECT-FOLDERdesktopProject folder creates, saves, opens, and relocates without semantic driftimplemented
TF-DESKTOP-DIRECT-EXPORTdesktopGeneric and Godot outputs are written safely to selected directoryimplemented
TF-DESKTOP-UNSAVED-CLOSEdesktopSave, Discard, and Cancel each produce the exact selected outcomeimplemented
TF-CROSS-HOST-WEB-TO-DESKTOPcross-hostElectron continues the web project with exact parityimplemented
TF-CROSS-HOST-DESKTOP-TO-WEBcross-hostWeb continues the desktop project with exact parityimplemented
TF-DATA-CUSTODYsharedUser sees storage location and owns a portable backup with no undeclared transferimplemented

Execution packages

IDEvidence gateStatus
WP-UPD001-SPECUpdate envelope, impact analysis, ADR-002, traceability, exclusions, and release gate pass reviewimplemented
WP-UPD001-CONTRACTSShared versioned schemas, parsers, canonical serializer, legacy migration, stable error taxonomy, safety limits, and fixtures pass pure and contract testsimplemented
WP-UPD001-HOST-ADAPTERSDomain modules have no browser or Electron dependency and host contract tests passimplemented
WP-UPD001-WEB-REPOSITORYIndexedDB library, version-1 migration, integer revisions, retention, quota recovery, snapshots, conflicts, and failure recovery pass actual-UI flowsimplemented
WP-UPD001-ARCHIVESArchive validation, deterministic canonical serialization, conflicts, migration, malicious fixtures, and exact round trip passimplemented
WP-UPD001-WEB-OFFLINEPublished-subpath PWA completes declared flows with network disconnectedimplemented
WP-UPD001-ELECTRON-SHELLPackaged portable Windows app launches with secure renderer and typed IPC checksimplemented
WP-UPD001-DESKTOP-FILESFolder lifecycle, entry fingerprints, external-change recovery, atomic writes, failures, direct exports, and unsaved close pass packaged Electron UI flowsimplemented
WP-UPD001-CROSS-HOSTBidirectional archive and output parity pass from real web and packaged Electron UIsimplemented
WP-UPD001-REGRESSIONAll affected RUN-MVP-001 baseline flows pass on affected hosts with zero severity 0-2 issuesimplemented

Deliberate exclusions

IDExpectationDisposition
OUT-UPD001-CLOUD-SYNCAutomatically synchronize projects between devicesFuture backend update after accepted customer promise and operating plan
OUT-UPD001-COLLABORATIONShare or co-edit projects in real timeNorth Star backend stage
OUT-UPD001-LIVE-LINKContinuously watch and update a running Godot projectFuture desktop expansion through formal update
OUT-UPD001-PROCESS-LAUNCHLaunch or control Godot and other local toolsFuture desktop expansion
OUT-UPD001-AUTO-UPDATEElectron updates itself automaticallyRevisit with signing and distribution plan
OUT-UPD001-INSTALLERInstall Electron through MSI, Store, or system packageFuture distribution update
OUT-UPD001-MAC-LINUXProvide portable macOS and Linux desktop buildsAdd only through host-specific verified update
OUT-UPD001-SIGNED-BINARYProvide a trusted signed Windows binaryRevisit when funding exists
OUT-UPD001-CUSTOM-PACK-FOLDERSWatch arbitrary local content-pack foldersContent ecosystem update
OUT-UPD001-HOST-DIVERGENCEAllow web and desktop projects or rendering rules to divergePermanently rejected unless a future ADR supersedes one-product-core principle

Review record

The canonical update folder contains the request, immutable baseline, impact analysis, exhaustive generated specification, architecture decisions, release arithmetic, and future evidence. Follow the Product Update Pipeline for every subsequent change.
